name: Salmon
summary: A fatty fish that's a leading source of the long-chain omega-3s EPA and DHA, plus one of the few natural food sources of vitamin D.
superfoodReason: Delivers the marine omega-3s EPA and DHA that the body uses directly, plus vitamin D and B12 — a combination few foods offer.

benefits.claim: Omega-3 fats support heart and triglycerides
benefits.mechanism: Salmon supplies EPA and DHA, long-chain omega-3 fatty acids that lower blood triglycerides, reduce inflammation, and are incorporated into cell membranes. Dietary guidelines recommend two servings of fatty fish weekly for cardiovascular health.

citations.title: Omega-3 Fatty Acids — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)

benefits.claim: A rare food source of vitamin D
benefits.mechanism: Vitamin D — which most people make from sunlight — is scarce in food, but fatty fish like salmon is one of the richest natural sources. Vitamin D helps the gut absorb calcium for bones and supports immune function.

citations.title: Vitamin D — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)

surprisingFacts.text: The pink colour comes from astaxanthin, a carotenoid antioxidant salmon get from eating krill and algae — farmed salmon are fed it to match the wild colour.
